prompt: Every Friday I do a weekly review — what shipped, what's stuck, what to focus on next week. I do it intuitively, takes 30-60 min, and the quality is uneven. I'd like to make it measurable and consistent so I can spot when I'm drifting. Help me design this as a real process.
assertions: Spec file written to the expected output path
verify_spec.py on the spec exits 0 (Gate 6 passes)
verify_spec.py --final on the spec exits 0 (all final assertions pass)
Cadence is named (Friday or weekly) somewhere in the spec
Output is a written review artifact (markdown / document), not a verb
Inputs include both the week's completed activity AND prior-week commitments
Process Health or Output metrics include at least one drift-detection signal (e.g. % commitments completed, focus-area continuity score, week-over-week velocity)
Mermaid block has at least 5 step nodes plus at least one GATE decision node
Edge Cases include 'commitment slipped' or equivalent recurring failure mode
Build handoff file exists and references the chosen target with appropriate capture mechanisms
Decision rules resolve on input alone (no 'use judgment' or vague criteria)
Session notes describe each of the 8 phases
